Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Marcus Webb's support follow-ups using search_emails with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "car delivery" or "car shipment" or "Honda Civic" (or similar). Expected discovery: Two emails from marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com to support@moveops.com, (1) email_email_ca010e9c9446 with subject "Checking in on my car delivery status," asking about his 2019 Honda Civic with no response received (is_read: true), and (2) email_email_87f575fcacf9 with subject "Second follow-up: I need an actual ETA for my car," CC'd to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, which is still unread (is_read: false). The second email is a child of the first (parent_id: email_email_ca010e9c9446). Together these confirm Marcus has sent at least two follow-ups without receiving a reply through the support inbox. This is a read/lookup action.
2. Search emails for the Road Runner Auto Transport delay notice using search_emails with a query related to "Road Runner" or "dispatch" or "vehicle delay" or "Indianapolis" or "carrier reassignment" (or similar). Expected discovery: An email from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com to Blessing Okafor reporting that Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic LX is sitting at the Indianapolis transfer hub awaiting carrier reassignment due to a driver call-off. Original ETA was April 8; revised delivery window is April 18–20 (tentative). This identifies Road Runner Auto Transport as the vendor handling the shipment and provides the current vehicle status. This is a read/lookup action.
3. Search emails for any MoveOps reply to Marcus Webb using search_emails with a query related to "Julian" and "Marcus" or "stepping in" or "Honda Civic" or "Indianapolis" (or similar). Expected discovery: Julian Brooks' email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com (CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) apologizing for the silence on Marcus's previous emails, confirming the car is delayed at the Indianapolis transfer hub after a Road Runner delay notice from April 11, and promising a fresh carrier status pull by 2:00 PM Pacific that day. This establishes what Marcus has already been told so Omar does not repeat or contradict Julian's message. This is a read/lookup action.
4. Search Slack for the Emilia Cruz piano callback note using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "scratched" or "piano" or "callback" (or similar), or retrieve history of channel C007 (#customer-support) using conversations_history. Expected discovery: Julian Brooks' message in #customer-support (C007, created 2026-04-13 at approximately 09:27): "Emilia Cruz just called in re: the scratched Steinway from yesterday's move. She was angry and honestly sounded embarrassed about how visible the damage is. Asked for a callback today from someone who can tell her whether MoveOps is taking responsibility or if she has to chase KeyMove herself. I told her we'd get someone back to her today. Leaving this here for pickup." Critical finding: reply_count is 0, meaning no one responded to Julian's handoff request, the promised same-day callback never happened. This is a read/lookup action.
5. Search emails for the KeyMove Specialty Transport damage report using search_emails with a query related to "KeyMove" or "Steinway" or "piano damage" or "Craig Nguyen" or "extraction" (or similar). Expected discovery: Craig Nguyen's email from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com (CC claims@keymove-specialty.com) sent to Blessing Okafor with a detailed damage report: instrument is a Steinway Model B grand piano in satin ebony, surface scratch approximately 4.5 to 5 inches on the bass side rim near the keyboard cheek block, damage occurred during extraction at the second-floor landing when the piano was rotated more aggressively than planned to clear the interior railing. This provides the specific damage details Omar needs for his response to Emilia. This is a read/lookup action.
6. Search Slack for Blessing Okafor's admission of operational fault regarding the piano damage using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Emilia Cruz" or "piano damage" or "own my part" or "extraction plan" or "site survey" (or similar), or retrieve history of channel C006 (#operations) using conversations_history. Expected discovery: Blessing Okafor's message in #operations (C006, created 2026-04-12 at approximately 06:45): she admits she green-lit the extraction plan off a thin assessment when she should have required a pre-move site survey before anyone touched the Steinway, and that the scratch was preventable if she had slowed things down and forced the survey. This establishes that MoveOps bears operational responsibility for the damage, not just the vendor KeyMove, which is the core question Emilia asked about. This is a read/lookup action.
7. Search or list Airtable relocation records for Marcus Webb using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Marcus Webb") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") and filtering results for "Marcus Webb" (or similar). Expected discovery: recMarcusWebbBrightloop, Marcus Webb, BrightLoop Analytics, Atlanta to Boston, Status: In Progress, Move Start: 2026-04-14, Move End: 2026-04-18, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements includes "Road Runner Auto Transport required as add-on" for 2019 Honda Civic (VIN: 2HGFC2F53KH123456) vehicle shipping at approximately $1,100 pending client approval, Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto. Important entity confusion risk: there is a separate completed relocation record (rec_reloc_webb) for a different Marcus Webb move (Atlanta to Denver via Heartland Movers, with Emeka Diallo as AM), the agent must distinguish between these two records and use recMarcusWebbBrightloop for the current active issue. This is a read/lookup action.
8. Search or list Airtable relocation records for Emilia Cruz using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Emilia Cruz") or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations") and filtering results for "Emilia Cruz" (or similar). Expected discovery: recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, Emilia Cruz, NorthWind Technologies, Chicago to Denver, Status: In Progress, Move Start: 2026-04-14, Move End: 2026-04-18, Assigned Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, Special Requirements includes "Partner's Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs) requires specialty piano movers" and "27-day lease overlap: Chicago lease ends May 15 but NorthWind needs employee in Denver by April 18." This confirms the relocation details, the coordinator responsible, and the Steinway context. This is a read/lookup action.
9. Search QuickBooks bills for the KeyMove insurance claim using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria filtering on vendor name "KeyMove" or description containing "piano" or "Steinway" or "Emilia" (or similar). Expected discovery: BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, $1,200 insurance claim rider for "Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction," vendor: KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), Doc Number: KM-44192-ICR, due date 2026-04-24, transaction date 2026-04-17. This reveals the financial exposure on the piano damage and confirms a claim process is already in motion. This is a read/lookup action.
10. Search CRM for BrightLoop account context using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find company_brightloop (type: Client, city: Boston, industry: Data Analytics, domain: brightloopanalytics.com). Then search deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "BrightLoop" or company_id "company_brightloop" (or similar) to find deal_brightloop_apr2026, amount: $11,350, stage: closedwon, dealname: "BrightLoop Analytics – April 2026 Relocations (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb)," with description detailing both relocations including Marcus's Honda Civic vehicle shipping via Road Runner and Simone's rush move via Swift. This provides the account context so Omar's response to Marcus aligns with what Mina Hashimoto (BrightLoop AM) has been communicating to the client. This is a read/lookup action.
11. Search CRM for NorthWind account context using crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar) to find company_northwind (type: Customer, city: Chicago, industry: Manufacturing Tech, description notes "quality/timeline issues" and "Catalina's account"). Then search deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "NorthWind" or company_id "company_northwind" (or similar) to find deal_northwind_denver_expansion, amount: $14,700, stage: qualifiedToBuy, covering Raj Patel (server lab with chain-of-custody) and Emilia Cruz (Steinway Model B piano). The agent should also discover through Slack, email, or Linear that NorthWind is currently a high-risk account: Pam Kowalski (NorthWind HR) sent a formal final-warning escalation citing accumulated service failures, Raj Patel had a chain-of-custody breach during his server move, and Emilia's piano damage is the latest incident. This context makes Omar's response to Emilia especially sensitive, he cannot afford to be dismissive or pass blame. This is a read/lookup action.
12. Search Linear for existing tickets related to both issues using linear_list_issues (or similar) to find: (1) issue linear_issue_c16357d188c6, #412, "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion" (assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1, covers Marcus Webb's vehicle issue alongside Simone Richter's housing complaint), (2) issue linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, #417, "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow" (assigned to Chloe Vance, specifically covers confirming the response path for Marcus after the unread Road Runner delay notice), and (3) issue linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, #482, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (assigned to David Chen, priority 1, explicitly references "Julian Brooks support note showing Emilia Cruz requested same-day callback and no owner picked it up"). These tickets determine whether new tracking tickets are needed or existing ones already cover the issues. This is a read/lookup action.
13. Search Slack for internal discussions about Marcus Webb and the BrightLoop vehicle issue using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "BrightLoop" or "Road Runner" or "vehicle" (or similar). Expected discoveries across multiple channels: (1) Mina Hashimoto's BrightLoop audit message in #customer-engagement (C002) flagging that Marcus's vehicle issue is still active and Road Runner's delay notice was never actioned, (2) Chloe Vance's directive in #operations (C006) calling out that the April 11 Road Runner notice was not actioned and Marcus has sent two follow-ups without a reply, directing that the delay gets acknowledged and an owner assigned for outbound communication, and (3) Julian Brooks' message in #customer-support (C007) confirming he drafted and sent a reply to Marcus. These messages give Omar the full internal picture of what has been discussed and decided about Marcus's situation. This is a read/lookup action.
14. Look up Marcus Webb's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus Webb" (or similar) to retrieve his email address marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com before sending the response. Note: multiple "Marcus Webb" contacts may exist in the system (BrightLoop, Canopy Health, Ironclad), the agent must use the BrightLoop Analytics contact. This is a read/lookup action.
15. Look up Emilia Cruz's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Emilia Cruz" (or similar) to retrieve her email address emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com before sending the response. This is a read/lookup action.
16. Send an email (via send_email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com with a status update on his 2019 Honda Civic shipment. The email must: (a) acknowledge the delay and the gap in communication on his previous support emails, (b) provide the current vehicle status, delayed at the Indianapolis transfer hub due to carrier reassignment after a driver call-off, with a revised delivery window of approximately April 18–20, (c) identify Road Runner Auto Transport as the vendor handling the shipment, and (d) provide a concrete next step or expected timeline for a follow-up update. The response should be consistent with what Julian Brooks already communicated to Marcus (acknowledgment of the Indianapolis delay, promise of carrier status pull) and should not contradict what Mina Hashimoto has been telling the BrightLoop account. This is a write action.
17. Send an email (via send_email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com addressing the piano damage situation. The email must: (a) acknowledge that MoveOps is aware of the damage to her Steinway Model B grand piano during the recent move, (b) confirm that MoveOps is taking responsibility for the issue, not pushing it to Emilia to chase KeyMove herself (which is specifically what she asked about in her call), (c) reference the insurance claim process for the damage (the $1,200 claim rider through KeyMove is already filed), and (d) provide a concrete next step for resolution. The tone should reflect the sensitivity of the NorthWind account given the recent escalations and Pam Kowalski's formal final warning about service quality. This is a write action.
18. Log a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_brightloop"]) on the BrightLoop account documenting Omar's support interaction regarding Marcus Webb's vehicle shipment follow-up. The engagement body should include: what was found (Road Runner Auto Transport delay at Indianapolis transfer hub, driver call-off, revised April 18–20 window, two support emails went unanswered before Julian stepped in, Julian's prior response and ETA promise), and what response was sent to Marcus. This gives the account manager (Mina Hashimoto) visibility into the support activity. This is a write action.
19. Log a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) on the NorthWind account documenting Omar's support interaction regarding Emilia Cruz's piano damage callback. The engagement body should include: what was found (Steinway Model B scratched during stairwell extraction at the second-floor landing, Blessing Okafor acknowledged operational fault, green-lit extraction without proper site survey, KeyMove filed $1,200 insurance claim rider via BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 due April 24, Julian's same-day callback promise went unfulfilled with reply_count 0 on the #customer-support handoff), and what response was sent to Emilia. This gives the account manager (Catalina Dubois) visibility into the support activity. This is a write action.
20. Create a Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue) or add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to an existing relevant ticket for the customer support follow-up actions performed during Omar's shift. Based on the investigation in OE 12, existing tickets #412 (BrightLoop audit, Mina), #417 (BrightLoop ops gaps, Chloe), and #482 (NorthWind retention, David) cover the operational tracking of these issues. If the agent determines existing tickets adequately cover the specific customer-facing follow-up, it should add a comment to the relevant ticket(s) documenting what Omar found and what responses were sent. If no existing ticket specifically tracks the T1 support response and the communication gap closure, create a new issue. This is a write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

This reveals the financial exposure on the piano damage and confirms a claim process is already in motion. This is a read/lookup action.
10. Search CRM for BrightLoop account context using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find company_brightloop (type: Client, city: Boston, industry: Data Analytics, domain: brightloopanalytics.com). Then search deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "BrightLoop" or company_id "company_brightloop" (or similar) to find deal_brightloop_apr2026, amount: $11,350, stage: closedwon, dealname: "BrightLoop Analytics – April 2026 Relocations (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb)," with description detailing both relocations including Marcus's Honda Civic vehicle shipping via Road Runner and Simone's rush move via Swift. This provides the account context so Omar's response to Marcus aligns with what Mina Hashimoto (BrightLoop AM) has been communicating to the client. This is a read/lookup action.
11. Search CRM for NorthWind account context using crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar) to find company_northwind (type: Customer, city: Chicago, industry: Manufacturing Tech, description notes "quality/timeline issues" and "Catalina's account"). Then search deals using crm_search_deals with dealname "NorthWind" or company_id "company_northwind" (or similar) to find deal_northwind_denver_expansion, amount: $14,700, stage: qualifiedToBuy, covering Raj Patel (server lab with chain-of-custody) and Emilia Cruz (Steinway Model B piano). The agent should also discover through Slack, email, or Linear that NorthWind is currently a high-risk account: Pam Kowalski (NorthWind HR) sent a formal final-warning escalation citing accumulated service failures, Raj Patel had a chain-of-custody breach during his server move, and Emilia's piano damage is the latest incident. This context makes Omar's response to Emilia especially sensitive, he cannot afford to be dismissive or pass blame. This is a read/lookup action.
12. Search Linear for existing tickets related to both issues using linear_list_issues (or similar) to find: (1) issue linear_issue_c16357d188c6, #412, "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion" (assigned to Mina Hashimoto, priority 1, covers Marcus Webb's vehicle issue alongside Simone Richter's housing complaint), (2) issue linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, #417, "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow" (assigned to Chloe Vance, specifically covers confirming the response path for Marcus after the unread Road Runner delay notice), and (3) issue linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, #482, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (assigned to David Chen, priority 1, explicitly references "Julian Brooks support note showing Emilia Cruz requested same-day callback and no owner picked it up"). These tickets determine whether new tracking tickets are needed or existing ones already cover the issues. This is a read/lookup action.
13. Search Slack for internal discussions about Marcus Webb and the BrightLoop vehicle issue using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "BrightLoop" or "Road Runner" or "vehicle" (or similar). Expected discoveries across multiple channels: (1) Mina Hashimoto's BrightLoop audit message in #customer-engagement (C002) flagging that Marcus's vehicle issue is still active and Road Runner's delay notice was never actioned, (2) Chloe Vance's directive in #operations (C006) calling out that the April 11 Road Runner notice was not actioned and Marcus has sent two follow-ups without a reply, directing that the delay gets acknowledged and an owner assigned for outbound communication, and (3) Julian Brooks' message in #customer-support (C007) confirming he drafted and sent a reply to Marcus. These messages give Omar the full internal picture of what has been discussed and decided about Marcus's situation. This is a read/lookup action.
14. Look up Marcus Webb's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Marcus Webb" (or similar) to retrieve his email address marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com before sending the response. Note: multiple "Marcus Webb" contacts may exist in the system (BrightLoop, Canopy Health, Ironclad), the agent must use the BrightLoop Analytics contact. This is a read/lookup action.
15. Look up Emilia Cruz's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "Emilia Cruz" (or similar) to retrieve her email address emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com before sending the response. This is a read/lookup action.
16. Send an email (via send_email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com with a status update on his 2019 Honda Civic shipment. The email must: (a) acknowledge the delay and the gap in communication on his previous support emails, (b) provide the current vehicle status, delayed at the Indianapolis transfer hub due to carrier reassignment after a driver call-off, with a revised delivery window of approximately April 18–20, (c) identify Road Runner Auto Transport as the vendor handling the shipment, and (d) provide a concrete next step or expected timeline for a follow-up update. The response should be consistent with what Julian Brooks already communicated to Marcus (acknowledgment of the Indianapolis delay, promise of carrier status pull) and should not contradict what Mina Hashimoto has been telling the BrightLoop account. This is a write action.
17. Send an email (via send_email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com addressing the piano damage situation. The email must: (a) acknowledge that MoveOps is aware of the damage to her Steinway Model B grand piano during the recent move, (b) confirm that MoveOps is taking responsibility for the issue, not pushing it to Emilia to chase KeyMove herself (which is specifically what she asked about in her call), (c) reference the insurance claim process for the damage (the $1,200 claim rider through KeyMove is already filed), and (d) provide a concrete next step for resolution. The tone should reflect the sensitivity of the NorthWind account given the recent escalations and Pam Kowalski's formal final warning about service quality. This is a write action.
18. Log a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_brightloop"]) on the BrightLoop account documenting Omar's support interaction regarding Marcus Webb's vehicle shipment follow-up. The engagement body should include: what was found (Road Runner Auto Transport delay at Indianapolis transfer hub, driver call-off, revised April 18–20 window, two support emails went unanswered before Julian stepped in, Julian's prior response and ETA promise), and what response was sent to Marcus. This gives the account manager (Mina Hashimoto) visibility into the support activity. This is a write action.
19. Log a CRM engagement (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) on the NorthWind account documenting Omar's support interaction regarding Emilia Cruz's piano damage callback. The engagement body should include: what was found (Steinway Model B scratched during stairwell extraction at the second-floor landing, Blessing Okafor acknowledged operational fault, green-lit extraction without proper site survey, KeyMove filed $1,200 insurance claim rider via BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 due April 24, Julian's same-day callback promise went unfulfilled with reply_count 0 on the #customer-support handoff), and what response was sent to Emilia. This gives the account manager (Catalina Dubois) visibility into the support activity. This is a write action.
20. Create a Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue) or add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to an existing relevant ticket for the customer support follow-up actions performed during Omar's shift. Based on the investigation in OE 12, existing tickets #412 (BrightLoop audit, Mina), #417 (BrightLoop ops gaps, Chloe), and #482 (NorthWind retention, David) cover the operational tracking of these issues. If the agent determines existing tickets adequately cover the specific customer-facing follow-up, it should add a comment to the relevant ticket(s) documenting what Omar found and what responses were sent. If no existing ticket specifically tracks the T1 support response and the communication gap closure, create a new issue. This is a write action.
